segments: U.S. dialysis and related lab services

supply_chain.dependencies: FMC manufactures dialysis supplies and equipment; DaVita has agreements requiring purchase of certain amounts of dialysis equipment, parts and supplies from FMC. One supplier's facility closure in September 2024 due to severe weather caused extensive damage and halted production of saline and peritoneal dialysate, requiring alternate sourcing.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain disruptions due to global events, geopolitical instability, trade disputes, natural disasters, severe weather events, product recalls, logistical challenges, fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates, varying regulatory requirements. Limited number of viable alternate suppliers for certain critical clinical supplies. Concentration risk with FMC as largest supplier.

regulatory_risks: Medicare and Medicaid coverage and reimbursement statutes, rules and regulations changes
Section 1115A of the Social Security Act - CMMI innovation models
Federal and state fraud waste and abuse laws (Anti-Kickback Statute, Stark Law, False Claims Act)
21st Century Cures Act requirements
Executive orders and presidential memoranda
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) compliance
Antitrust and competition laws
Corporate practice of medicine laws
HIPAA privacy and security regulations
No Surprises Act and price transparency regulations
State certificate of need programs
FDA regulations
Trade restrictions and export controls
State ballot initiatives limiting dialysis provider payments (e.g., California AB 290)
State minimum wage laws (e.g., California SB 525)
FTC rule banning post-employment non-compete clauses
EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)
UK GDPR
Brazilian Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD)
Saudi Arabia Personal Data Protection Law
Data Security Law of People's Republic of China (DSL)
California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA)
State data security and breach notification laws
Medicare provider enrollment requirements and potential revocation/suspension
State Medicaid program changes and payment rate reductions
CMS Quality Incentive Program (QIP) penalties
Medicare bad debt claim audits and payment adjustments
Charitable premium assistance restrictions
Changes to Medicare Advantage (MA) benchmark structure and payment rates

sanction_risks: Impact of geopolitical conflicts (Russia-Ukraine, Israel-Gaza) on supply chain and operations
Trade disputes affecting supply chain and regulatory requirements
Patriot Act and Executive Order No. 13224 on Terrorist Financing compliance
